19.gl: a collective name for a flock (of sheep or goats); also figuratively (of men)
19.dv: or צאוֹן; (Psalm 144:13), from an unused root meaning to migrate;

19.gl: beef cattle or an animal of the ox family of either gender (as used for plowing); collectively, a herd
19.kj: beeve, bull ([phrase] -ock), [phrase] calf, [phrase] cow, great (cattle), [phrase] heifer, herd, kine, ox.

22.gl: splendor (as conspicuous); also a gazelle (as beautiful)
22.dv: from H6638 (צָבָה) in the sense of prominence;

22.kj: alike, at all (once), both, likewise, only, (al-) together, withal.

22.gl: pure (in a physical, chemical, ceremonial or moral sense)
an opening, i.e. door or gate

23.gl: properly, leanness, i.e. (figuratively) limitation; only adverbial, merely, or conjunctional, although
23.kj: but, even, except, howbeit howsoever, at the least, nevertheless, nothing but, notwithstanding, only, save, so (that), surely, yet (so), in any wise.
23.dv: the same as H7534 (רַק) as a noun;

23.gl: blood (as that which when shed causes death) of man or an animal; by analogy, the juice of the grape; figuratively (especially in the plural) bloodshed (i.e. drops of blood)
to spill forth (blood, a libation, liquid metal; or even a solid, i.e. to mound up); also (figuratively) to expend (life, soul, complaint, money, etc.); intensively, to sprawl out
23.kj: cast (up), gush out, pour (out), shed(-der, out), slip.

23.dv: from an unused root probably meaning to be firm;

23.gl: water; figuratively, juice; by euphemism, urine, semen
23.dv: dual of a primitive noun (but used in a singular sense);
